sections.content:
Angles and Parallel Lines

A full turn is 360°; a straight line is 180°; a right angle is 90°. When a line (transversal) crosses two parallel lines, it creates several angle pairs:

  • Corresponding angles — equal (same side, same position)
  • Alternate angles — equal (Z-shape)
  • Co-interior angles — add to 180° (C-shape)

The interior angles of any triangle add to 180°. The interior angles of a quadrilateral add to 360°. For any polygon: sum of interior angles = (n − 2) × 180°, where n is the number of sides.

🔑 Key formulae: Area of triangle = ½ × base × height. Area of circle = πr². Circumference = 2πr. Volume of cuboid = l × w × h. Volume of cylinder = πr²h. Pythagoras: a² + b² = c² (right-angled triangle, c is hypotenuse).

Pythagoras' Theorem

In any right-angled triangle, the square of the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle) equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides: a² + b² = c².

To find the hypotenuse: c = √(a² + b²). Example: a = 3, b = 4 → c = √(9 + 16) = √25 = 5. (The 3-4-5 triangle is the most famous Pythagorean triple.)

To find a shorter side: a = √(c² − b²). Example: c = 13, b = 5 → a = √(169 − 25) = √144 = 12.

Trigonometry in Right-Angled Triangles

For a right-angled triangle with angle θ, the three sides are labelled relative to θ: opposite (O), adjacent (A), and hypotenuse (H). The three trig ratios are:

  • sin θ = O/H
  • cos θ = A/H
  • tan θ = O/A

Mnemonic: SOH CAH TOA. To find a missing angle, use the inverse function: θ = sin⁻¹(O/H), etc.

Circles and Arcs

A circle with radius r has area πr² and circumference 2πr. An arc is a portion of the circumference; a sector is a 'pie slice'. For a sector with angle θ (in degrees):

  • Arc length = (θ/360) × 2πr
  • Sector area = (θ/360) × πr²

A chord is a straight line across the circle. The perpendicular from the centre to a chord bisects it — useful for finding missing lengths.

questions.question: A right-angled triangle has legs 5 cm and 12 cm. What is the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: c² = 5² + 12² = 25 + 144 = 169. c = √169 = 13 cm. This is the 5-12-13 Pythagorean triple.

questions.question: What is the sum of interior angles of a hexagon?
sin 30° = ?
questions.explanation: sin 30° = 0.5 = ½. The exact values sin 30° = ½, sin 45° = 1/√2, sin 60° = √3/2 are worth memorising.

questions.question: A circle has radius 5 cm. What is its area? (Use π ≈ 3.14)
